title: #eng-runtime: 1740144420
preview:
Hiro Tanaka: seeing a decode p95 regression when enabling the new INT8 profile on llama3.1-70b-instruct variant x1. baseline p95 decode ~38ms/token, INT8 profile jumps to ~52ms/token on A100-80GB. encode looks fine. anyone else seeing this?

Noah Patel: which profile id? and what runtime build?

text:
Hiro Tanaka: seeing a decode p95 regression when enabling the new INT8 profile on llama3.1-70b-instruct variant x1. baseline p95 decode ~38ms/token, INT8 profile jumps to ~52ms/token on A100-80GB. encode looks fine. anyone else seeing this?

Noah Patel: which profile id? and what runtime build?

Hiro Tanaka: profile ref `qpl:llama3.1-70b-x1:int8_w8a16:2025-02-19.3` (aka v3). runtime `runtime@b7f4c2a` from yesterday. route is pinned, no other changes.

Sofia Ivanova: is it only when continuous batching kicks in? (i.e. queue depth > 1)

Hiro Tanaka: happens even at qd=1. perf suite run `decode_only` shows it.

Omar El-Sayed: any chance we’re invalidating KV cache layout due to different pack/unpack? INT8 weights shouldn’t touch KV but we do some alignment changes for fused kernels.

Hiro Tanaka: grabbed nsight systems trace. looks like we’re not taking the fused attention kernel on deco...

title: #eng-runtime: 1739824101
preview:
Omar El-Sayed: Need to settle the hit/miss definitions today so Samir can finish counters + Nadia can lock schema. Main confusion: KV cache hit vs prefix cache hit, and what “partial reuse” means esp multi-turn chat.

Samir Qureshi: +1. Implementation wise I can emit (a) tokens reused (skipped compute) and (b) token...
text:
Omar El-Sayed: Need to settle the hit/miss definitions today so Samir can finish counters + Nadia can lock schema. Main confusion: KV cache hit vs prefix cache hit, and what “partial reuse” means esp multi-turn chat.

Samir Qureshi: +1. Implementation wise I can emit (a) tokens reused (skipped compute) and (b) tokens eligible. But people keep asking for “request hit rate” which gets weird w partial.

Noah Patel: Let’s be super explicit about numerator/denominator and *stage*. KV cache is about reuse across turns within a session (or same route) at decode-time, but prefix cache is prefill-time reuse of prompt prefix. If we mix the two it’ll be garbage.

Sofia Ivanova: Also continuous batching complicates attribution. Prefill for multiple reqs in one batch, we need per-request counts. We can’t have “hit” mean “batch-level cache was warm”.

Omar El-Sayed: Proposal:
- Prefix cache metrics...

title: #eng-runtime: 1739390012
preview:
Noah Patel: Heads up from last night’s rollback drill: we saw a noticeable p95/p99 spike right after rollback when we invalidate caches. Concern: rollback is “fix the regression” but we might temporarily make latency worse.

Noah Patel: Specifically: rollback flips optimization config version, runtime applies + does...
text:
Noah Patel: Heads up from last night’s rollback drill: we saw a noticeable p95/p99 spike right after rollback when we invalidate caches. Concern: rollback is “fix the regression” but we might temporarily make latency worse.

Noah Patel: Specifically: rollback flips optimization config version, runtime applies + does cache invalidation (prefix/KV + some routing caches). Workers drop hot prefixes and we get a burst of cold prefill.

Omar El-Sayed: yep, that matches what I’ve been seeing in local profiling. invalidation is effectively forcing prefill on first N requests. if rollback happens under load, we get queueing + p99 balloons.

Sofia Ivanova: do we have numbers from the gameday? how bad was the spike + how long did it last

Noah Patel: from the drill: steady-state p95 ~240ms, during rollback window p95 ~410ms for ~2-3 mins. p99 went from ~900ms to ~2.6s at peak, then recovered. er...

title: #eng-runtime: 1739305589
preview:
Noah Patel: heads up for quant profile library runtime wiring — a few concerns I want to sync on:
1) determinism (same request+route should resolve same profile every time)
2) cache interactions (prefix/KV cache + batching) w/ mixed quant modes
3) traces/logs: how do we *prove* profile applied vs fallback

Sofia Iva...
text:
Noah Patel: heads up for quant profile library runtime wiring — a few concerns I want to sync on:
1) determinism (same request+route should resolve same profile every time)
2) cache interactions (prefix/KV cache + batching) w/ mixed quant modes
3) traces/logs: how do we *prove* profile applied vs fallback

Sofia Ivanova: +1 esp on determinism. if control plane returns `recommended` alias and that changes under us, we’ll have "same config" but different kernels/weights day-to-day.

Jordan Lee: determinism requirement from platform side: resolved settings must be deterministic per route config snapshot. For v1 we should treat `quantization_profile_ref` as pinned (profile_id + version), not a moving alias.

Noah Patel: ok so runtime should only see a fully-resolved `profile_id@version`? (or `disabled`)

Jordan Lee: yep. policy engine resolves route cfg -> concrete profile ref. if alias e...

title: #eng-runtime: 1738865402
preview:

Ethan Park: yeah, two things we’ve hit: (1) `eos_token_id` mismatch when upstream publishes multiple eos candidates, and (2) ou...
text:
Dev Patel: anyone remember the gotchas when adding a new upstream tokenizer to hosted? qwen2.5 looks fine in eval harness but I’m worried about stop sequences + special tokens in streaming

Ethan Park: yeah, two things we’ve hit: (1) `eos_token_id` mismatch when upstream publishes multiple eos candidates, and (2) our OpenAI-compat path strips leading spaces differently. check the tokenizer config in registry and make sure we set `strip_leading_space=false` if needed

Aisha Rahman: also for evals, run the internal prompt set with the *exact* stop sequence config we ship. we had a false pass last time because harness used default stops but prod route used `

` + `</s>`

Connor OBrien: rollout side: please ensure router policy has fallback #2 to a cross-family safe default (we’ve been using `rw/llama-3.1-70b-instruct`), otherwise incident response is painful if the whole family hits OOM
...

title: #eng-runtime: 1738453311
preview:
Jada Williams: Been digging into the latest blended $/1M output token spikes on a couple long-context routes. We’re seeing the usual pattern: output tokens flat-ish, but per-request GPU time up because prefill is huge. We need a *runtime* signal that explains this better than “avg input tokens”.

Jada Williams: Prop...
text:
Jada Williams: Been digging into the latest blended $/1M output token spikes on a couple long-context routes. We’re seeing the usual pattern: output tokens flat-ish, but per-request GPU time up because prefill is huge. We need a *runtime* signal that explains this better than “avg input tokens”.

Jada Williams: Proposal: add a “prefill/decode ratio” metric (or prefill share) emitted per request and aggregated hourly. It would help explain unit cost shifts when prompts get longer or cache hit rate changes.

Allison Grant: +1. For the dashboard folks keep asking “why did $/output token jump when output length didn’t change” and the answer is almost always prefill dominated.

Logan Wright: Agree. But we should be super explicit on definition so we don’t end up with 3 different ratios.

Nadia Rahman: What exactly do you mean by prefill/decode ratio here? Tokens? time? FLOPs proxy?

Jada W...

title: #eng-runtime: 1737684455
preview:
Nadia Rahman: we need a batching utilization metric out of runtime asap. looking at the early unit econ views, we’re seeing $/1M output tokens drift but we can’t tell if it’s “more expensive GPUs” vs “worse batching”. cache hit rate helps a bit but doesn’t explain the spikes.

Nadia Rahman: asking eng-runtime: can w...
text:
Nadia Rahman: we need a batching utilization metric out of runtime asap. looking at the early unit econ views, we’re seeing $/1M output tokens drift but we can’t tell if it’s “more expensive GPUs” vs “worse batching”. cache hit rate helps a bit but doesn’t explain the spikes.

Nadia Rahman: asking eng-runtime: can we add a stable metric for “how full were we” during decode/prefill? something finance/product can interpret w/o reading kernel traces.

Logan Wright: +1. in pipeline we can join whatever you emit, but right now we only have request-level batch_size-ish from gateway logs, and that doesn’t reflect continuous batching behavior.

Allison Grant: what exactly do you mean by utilization? avg batch size? gpu occupancy? those are v diff and can be misleading.

Nadia Rahman: fair. not asking for GPU occupancy (too noisy + hardware-specific). i want something like “tokens processed pe...

title: #eng-runtime: 1737665440
preview:
Diego Santos: quick q for audit logging coverage — what runtime-level actions are we calling “sensitive”? thinking model enable/disable, fleet pool bindings, etc. do we need audit events for these or do we treat as internal ops?

Hanae Suzuki: +1, security side assumption has been: any action that changes (a) what m...
text:
Diego Santos: quick q for audit logging coverage — what runtime-level actions are we calling “sensitive”? thinking model enable/disable, fleet pool bindings, etc. do we need audit events for these or do we treat as internal ops?

Hanae Suzuki: +1, security side assumption has been: any action that changes (a) what model can serve traffic, (b) where traffic can go, or (c) who can access capacity => sensitive.

Marcus Lin: yep. also anything that changes policy enforcement (eg bypassing allow-lists), even if done via runtime config.

Ethan Park: to clarify, “runtime-level” here = changes in serving layer config (model registry flags, pool allocations) vs control plane policy? where are these changes initiated today?

Diego Santos: mix. some via control plane (rollouts/policy), but some are “runtime admin” endpoints (mostly used by oncall) for: 
- enable/disable model version in a region...

title: #eng-runtime: 1737601104
preview:

In the unit econ views it swings +/-20pp day to day for the same model+region, but latency + GPU-seconds don’t move. Before we bake this into the dashboard “cost drivers” section, can we sanity check what we’re actually measuring?
...
text:
Nadia Rahman: heads up: cache hit rate signal in metering looks noisy/possibly wrong. In the unit econ views it swings +/-20pp day to day for the same model+region, but latency + GPU-seconds don’t move. Before we bake this into the dashboard “cost drivers” section, can we sanity check what we’re actually measuring?

Caleb Johnson: +1. I pulled yesterday’s hourly aggregates and the cache_hit_rate dropped to ~0.12 for `llama-70b` in us-east for ~3 hours, but request volume + output tokens were normal. That feels like instrumentation drop vs real cache behavior.

Logan Wright: what’s the exact definition we’re using right now? is it (cache_hits / (cache_hits+cache_misses)) at request-level? or token-level? I’ve seen both definitions float around.

Nadia Rahman: current pipeline assumes token-level: `cached_prefill_tokens / total_prefill_tokens` (from runtime stats). But I’m not confident...

title: #eng-runtime: 1737369021
preview:
Noah Patel: starting a thread on runtime side for safe apply/rollback. main questions: idempotency semantics, worker cache invalidation, and what we do under partial rollout / mixed state.

Omar El-Sayed: +1. esp worried about KV/prefix cache. if config toggles caching/batching knobs, a rollback could leave workers ...
text:
Noah Patel: starting a thread on runtime side for safe apply/rollback. main questions: idempotency semantics, worker cache invalidation, and what we do under partial rollout / mixed state.

Omar El-Sayed: +1. esp worried about KV/prefix cache. if config toggles caching/batching knobs, a rollback could leave workers with stale cache assumptions.

Sofia Ivanova: also the batching heuristics changes are stateful-ish (scheduler params). we need to be clear whether “apply v123” is best-effort vs guaranteed across all workers before controller moves on.

Benji Okafor: from control plane side, rollout controller will treat runtime as eventually consistent but needs a strong signal: “applied checkpoint reached for shard X” + ability to retry safely.

Noah Patel: proposal: runtime applier is idempotent per (scope, config_version). Think of it as:
- input: `tenant_id`, `model`, `region`, `confi...

title: #eng-runtime: 1732109876
preview:
Ava Chen: Nightly perf job artifacts size spiked — nsys captures for the KV-heavy smoke runs are ~1.6GB each and uploads are timing out. Seen this on the new kvcache evictions tests. Anyone else?
Samir Patel: Yup. We capture a lot (full cuda context + nvtx + memory). We should trim capture scope and only collect ful...
text:
Ava Chen: Nightly perf job artifacts size spiked — nsys captures for the KV-heavy smoke runs are ~1.6GB each and uploads are timing out. Seen this on the new kvcache evictions tests. Anyone else?
Samir Patel: Yup. We capture a lot (full cuda context + nvtx + memory). We should trim capture scope and only collect full nsys when the run looks anomalous. :eyes:
Liam O'Neill: What exactly is CI running atm? can you paste the command?
Ava Chen: Current CI snippet (from circle job):
```bash
nsys profile --output ${ARTIFACT} --trace=cuda,nvtx --force-overwrite 
 --stats=true 
 --stop-on-exit 
 python run_kvcache_smoke.py --iters 100
```
Samir Patel: Ok. A few levers: 1) restrict trace range (e.g. --capture-range=nvtx) 2) disable heavy memory tracking 3) use nsys --compress or switch to perfetto for lightweight timeline. Also consider sampling only when kv miss rate > threshold.
Julie Ramos: ...

title: #eng-runtime: 1712105432
preview: Maria Chen: Heads-up — planning a gated rollout for kernel-quant v3.2 tonight in us-west (change window 02:00-03:00 PT). DeployBot will run preflight smoke + perf canary. Freeze engine non-essential merges at 00:30 PT. Please ack if you can watch metrics and own rollback if needed. Release notes draft: https://redwo...
text:
Maria Chen: Heads-up — planning a gated rollout for kernel-quant v3.2 tonight in us-west (change window 02:00-03:00 PT). DeployBot will run preflight smoke + perf canary. Freeze engine non-essential merges at 00:30 PT. Please ack if you can watch metrics and own rollback if needed. Release notes draft: https://redwood.internal/releases/kernel-quant/v3.2 :memo:

Kevin Liu: ack. I'll monitor p50/p95, error-rate, and GPU temp dashboards. Posting links in a bit.

DeployBot: Preflight queued: [smoke, kv-cache-sanity, perf-runner]. Expected checks ~10m. Will report back.

Jamie Ortiz: Quick question — what are our rollback thresholds? Proposing: p95 latency +30ms sustained for 5m OR error-rate >0.5% sustained for 5m OR kv-miss-rate spike >2x baseline. If any hit -> immediate roll to v3.1 pin.

Akshay Rao: Adding metrics to the watchlist: kv-miss-rate, batching-latency, gpu-temp, warmstart-t...
